title: Bank of America Corporation (BAC) Intrinsic Value — Opus 4.6 Analysis
description: Opus 4.6 independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of Bank of America Corporation (BAC).

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y10)
fcfGrowth.rationale: Bank of America Corporation is projected to grow FCF at 5% annually based on competitive dynamics and historical performance.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: A 10.0% WACC reflects Bank of America Corporation's risk profile, sector volatility, and balance sheet quality.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: A 2.0% terminal rate for long-run growth convergence toward nominal GDP.

thesisTitle: Bank of America Corporation: A Fundamental Assessment
thesisParagraphs: Bank of America Corporation (BAC) is an S&P 500 constituent with established competitive advantages and consistent cash flow generation. The company's market position provides pricing power and earnings stability.

My DCF applies a 5% FCF growth rate reflecting the company's historical compounding ability, with a 10.0% discount rate capturing sector-specific risks. The valuation is sensitive to growth assumptions — investors should stress-test these inputs.
verdictFooter: Opus 4.6 independent analysis. Data via public filings (Mar 20, 2026).

faq.question: What is Bank of America Corporation's intrinsic value according to Opus?
faq.answer: Based on a 10-year DCF with 5% growth and 10.0% WACC, Opus values BAC at $50.38/share vs $44.00 current price.

faq.question: Is BAC overvalued or undervalued?
faq.answer: Opus's DCF suggests BAC is undervalued with a +14.5% margin of safety.

faq.question: Should I trade based on this?
faq.answer: No. This is not financial advice. DCF models are sensitive to assumptions. Use as one data point among many.
